question: What is the name of the Justice League's headquarters building in Washington?
explanation: The Hall of Justice is the Justice League's base, built into the cliffs above the Potomac River in Washington.

question: Which villain rules the world of Apokolips and is one of the Justice League's greatest foes?
explanation: Darkseid is the tyrant of Apokolips who commands armies of Parademons and seeks the Anti-Life Equation.

question: What is Arthur Curry's royal title?
explanation: Arthur Curry — Aquaman — is the King of Atlantis and commands the sea and its creatures.

question: What object does Darkseid's army search for that can open portals to Apokolips?
explanation: A Mother Box is an ancient living computer from Apokolips that can open Boom Tubes — portals through space.

question: What is the real name of the Flash who is a member of the Justice League?
explanation: Barry Allen is the Flash and forensic scientist who serves as a founding member of the Justice League.

question: What is the name of Wonder Woman's golden weapon that compels truth?
explanation: The Lasso of Truth is Wonder Woman's weapon — anyone caught in it cannot speak a lie.

question: Hal Jordan is the Justice League's Green Lantern. What powers his ring?
explanation: Hal Jordan's Green Lantern ring is powered by willpower — the stronger his will, the more powerful his constructs.

question: Victor Stone combines human and machine. By what hero name is he known?
explanation: Victor Stone is Cyborg — half-human, half-machine — whose built-in technology allows him to interface with any system.

question: J'onn J'onzz is the Martian Manhunter. Which planet is he the last survivor of?
explanation: J'onn J'onzz is the last survivor of Mars, making him a telepath and shapeshifter who carries his world's memory.

question: What shape is the Justice League's conference table in the Hall of Justice?
explanation: The round table has eight equal seats with no seat at the head, representing that every member's voice counts equally.

question: What is Superman's human name?
explanation: Clark Kent is Superman's human name; his Kryptonian birth name is Kal-El.

question: What is Batman's real name?
explanation: Bruce Wayne is Batman — a detective and gadget expert who uses intelligence rather than superpowers.

question: From which island did Wonder Woman come before joining the wider world?
explanation: Wonder Woman — Diana — came from Themyscira, the hidden island home of the Amazons, ruled by Queen Hippolyta.

question: What do Darkseid's winged soldiers who serve him look like?
explanation: Parademons are Darkseid's winged armored soldiers with burning red eyes who pour from Boom Tubes.

question: The alien Brainiac collects cities by doing what to them?
options: Miniaturizing them and sealing them in bottles
explanation: Brainiac uses shrink-ray technology to miniaturize cities and keeps them in glass bottles aboard his ship.

question: Which Kryptonian city was stolen and miniaturized by Brainiac and kept in a bottle?
explanation: Kandor was a great Kryptonian city that Brainiac miniaturized and keeps in a glass bottle on his ship.

question: What color does Sinestro's power ring glow?
explanation: Sinestro wields a yellow fear-powered ring, which is effective against the Green Lanterns' willpower-based rings.

question: In which city does the Flash, Barry Allen, live and protect?
explanation: Barry Allen protects Central City as the Flash and works there as a forensic scientist.

question: In which city does Hal Jordan live as the Green Lantern?
explanation: Hal Jordan grew up in Coast City, near Ferris Aircraft, where he became a test pilot before becoming Green Lantern.

question: What terrifying thing would happen if Darkseid found the Anti-Life Equation?
options: Free will would vanish from every mind on Earth
explanation: The Anti-Life Equation is a mathematical truth that, if used by Darkseid, would erase free will from every mind on Earth.

question: What does a Boom Tube do?
options: It creates a deafening noise to stun enemies
It opens a portal through space
explanation: A Boom Tube is a crackling portal that opens a doorway through space, used by Darkseid and his forces to travel between worlds.

question: The Legion of Doom is led by which villain?
explanation: Lex Luthor organizes the Legion of Doom, assembling villains specifically chosen to defeat individual Justice League members.

question: Which villain from the Legion of Doom is a giant telepathic gorilla?
explanation: Gorilla Grodd is a giant telepathic gorilla who can rewrite what people see and feel, making him especially dangerous.

question: Which Legion of Doom villain is an armored deep-sea hunter who fights Aquaman?
explanation: Black Manta wears armor built to withstand deep ocean pressure and is one of Aquaman's most persistent enemies.

question: Which city did the Martian Manhunter quietly live in as an ordinary person before joining the League?
explanation: J'onn J'onzz lived in Middleton, Colorado for years under a human identity before joining the Justice League.

question: What weapon does Aquaman carry?
explanation: Aquaman wields a silver trident, which channels his power and serves as both weapon and symbol of his kingship.

question: What special ability does the Martian Manhunter have besides shapeshifting?
explanation: J'onn J'onzz is a telepath who can read minds, sense emotions, and locate people by their thoughts.

question: During the Legion of Doom battle, what did Batman secretly prepare to defeat Luthor's Kryptonite armor?
options: A signal to overload the containment field's power regulator
explanation: Batman prepared a precise frequency signal that overloads the Kryptonite containment field's regulator, disabling it.

question: What does Green Lantern create using his power ring?
options: Physical constructs made of green light
explanation: Green Lantern's ring can create any physical construct the wearer can imagine out of green energy, powered by willpower.

question: What is the name of Batman's home city?
explanation: Bruce Wayne is deeply connected to Gotham City, which he has dedicated his life to protecting.

question: What did J'onn J'onzz bring to the common room shelf at the Hall of Justice?
explanation: J'onn placed his single framed photograph — the only object he brought from Mars — on the shared shelf as a sign of belonging.

question: During the rescue mission against Darkseid, how many people were taken by Parademons in the first seven minutes?
explanation: Two hundred people were seized by Darkseid's Parademons across twelve locations in the first seven minutes.

question: How did Wonder Woman's Lasso of Truth help against Gorilla Grodd's illusions?
options: It reflected his powers back at him
explanation: The Lasso of Truth is truth itself, and illusions cannot survive in the presence of truth — Grodd's visions dissolved immediately.

question: Which two Justice League members are described as best friends who argue about pancake syrup?
explanation: Barry Allen (the Flash) and Hal Jordan (Green Lantern) became best friends despite their very different personalities.

question: What did Cyborg do during the Justice League's rescue mission to keep the final Boom Tube open?
options: Held it open with his hands
Asked Green Lantern to hold it
Built a machine to keep it running
Used his own power systems as a conductor
explanation: Victor Stone used his own built-in power systems as a conductor to keep the Boom Tube open past its natural closing time.

question: What did Wonder Woman notice about her bedroom window when she moved into the Hall of Justice?
options: It had a view of the Potomac River
explanation: Diana chose the room with a view of the Potomac River and placed her golden tiara on the windowsill.

question: During the Brainiac disagreement, what combined plan did the League settle on for fighting him?
options: In the upper atmosphere between Earth and space
explanation: The team combined Superman's plan (going high) and Batman's plan (using Earth) by fighting in the upper atmosphere.

question: What is Superman's home planet called?
explanation: Superman — Kal-El — was born on the planet Krypton before being sent to Earth as a baby.

question: What speech did Batman give at the Justice League's one-year anniversary?
options: You kept your word. All of you.
explanation: Batman's speech was simply 'You kept your word. All of you.' — brief words that meant a great deal coming from Bruce Wayne.

question: What does J'onn J'onzz — the Martian Manhunter — keep as the only object brought from Mars?
explanation: J'onn carries a single framed photograph — the only object he brought from Mars — which he eventually places on the shared shelf at the Hall.
